opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: The room is completely spinning, and I can't stop retching every time I try to move my head.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: I got back from a rough day on a fishing boat about four hours ago and it hasn't stopped — every time I move my head I get violently dizzy and throw up, and I can't keep any fluid down at all.

symptom_narrative.onset: started about 6 hours ago while I was out on a turbulent deep-sea fishing charter
symptom_narrative.duration: about 6 hours, and 4 hours after getting back on dry land it is still going
symptom_narrative.character: began with a queasy churning stomach and a lot of yawning, then cold sweats, watery mouth and a spinning, whirling dizziness; now any head movement sets off intense spinning and vomiting
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: severe — I have to lie dead still with my eyes shut, and even lifting my head makes me retch
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: the spinning is in my head and I feel it all through my stomach
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant while still, but any movement of my head makes it dramatically worse
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: lying completely still with my eyes closed; vomiting gives a few seconds of relief and then it comes back
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: any movement or turning of my head
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: cold sweating, pale and clammy, lots of saliva, and I tried a ginger tablet but brought it straight back up
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I've felt queasy on boats before but never anything like this
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I'm stuck in bed and can't stand or walk to the bathroom safely because of the dizziness

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: none regularly; tried one over-the-counter ginger tablet today and vomited it straight back up

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives alone, so there is no one at home to help; worried about not being able to keep any water down

objective_data_offered.value: looks very pale with sweat visible on the forehead; lying rigidly still with eyes closed, holding a bowl
cannot keep any fluid down — has vomited everything, including a ginger tablet, over the last few hours
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: self-report; a key marker of dehydration risk but not clinician-verified

objective_data_offered.value: the vomit in the bucket is clear and yellowish (bile), with no blood or coffee-ground appearance
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient description of vomitus appearance; relevant to excluding a GI bleed but not clinician-verified
